title: Day 24 Interview Prep — Full Mock Loop
description: 10+ questions covering all five mock interview rounds: JavaScript, React, machine coding, project discussion, and debugging. Answer out loud under timed conditions — final interview prep before graduation.

questions.question: Round 1 — Explain the event loop. What runs first: Promise.then or setTimeout?
questions.whatInterviewerTests: Async fundamentals — first question in most frontend loops.
questions.sampleAnswer: JS is single-threaded. Sync code runs on the call stack. When empty, all microtasks (Promise.then) drain before the next macrotask (setTimeout). That is why Promise.then runs before setTimeout(0).

questions.question: Round 1 — What is a closure? Implement debounce behavior conceptually.
questions.whatInterviewerTests: Closure fluency plus practical pattern from Week 2.
questions.sampleAnswer: Closure: inner function accesses outer variables after outer returns. Debounce(fn, ms) returns a wrapper that clears previous timeout and sets new one — fn fires only after ms silence. Used in movie search to reduce API calls.

questions.question: Round 1 — Explain this, call, bind, and apply with one example each.
questions.whatInterviewerTests: Explicit this binding — still asked in senior loops.
questions.sampleAnswer: call invokes immediately with this: greet.call(user). apply same but args as array. bind returns new function with bound this — useful for class handlers. Arrow functions inherit lexical this — no own binding.
followUps: Lost this in React class component?

questions.question: Round 2 — useEffect vs event handlers — when do you use each?
questions.whatInterviewerTests: Most common React mistake — effects for everything.
questions.sampleAnswer: useEffect for external systems — fetch on mount, subscriptions, DOM sync. Event handlers for user clicks and form submits. Never useEffect for data derivable in render. Always deps array and cleanup.

questions.question: Round 2 — Context vs Redux vs RTK Query — give me the decision tree.
questions.whatInterviewerTests: Architecture fluency across all Week 3 state tools.
questions.sampleAnswer: Context: theme, locale — rare updates, memoized value. Redux Toolkit: complex client state with many actions and slices. RTK Query: server cache, mutations, invalidation. They coexist — RTK Query does not replace Redux for UI flags.

questions.question: Round 2 — When do you use useRef vs useState?
questions.whatInterviewerTests: Hook selection — DOM access vs mutable value without re-render.
questions.sampleAnswer: useRef for DOM nodes — input.focus(). Also mutable values that should not trigger re-render — timer ids, prev props, render count. useState when UI must update on change.

questions.question: Round 3 — You have 60 minutes to build a Movie Search app. First 10 minutes?
questions.whatInterviewerTests: Machine coding process — same as Day 21–22 but tighter timer.
questions.sampleAnswer: Min 1–3: confirm API endpoint, required fields, detail page or modal. Min 4–5: Movie and SearchResult types. Min 6–8: SearchBar + grid layout with hardcoded data. Min 9–10: debounced fetch wired. I talk through trade-offs as I go.

questions.question: Round 4 — Explain how FrontForge renders a lesson from JSON.
questions.whatInterviewerTests: Project discussion — can you explain your learning platform architecture?
questions.sampleAnswer: Curriculum lives in content/dayNN/*.json — meta, lesson, quiz, etc. Zod schemas validate at build/CI. lesson.json blocks array has typed blocks — heading, code, checklist. BlockRenderer maps block.type to React component. Adding a day means authoring JSON, not new page components.

questions.question: Round 4 — What was the hardest challenge building FrontForge and how did you solve it?
questions.whatInterviewerTests: Authentic struggle + resolution — interviewers probe for honesty.
questions.sampleAnswer: Keeping lesson JSON schemas and renderer in sync — adding accordion blocks required schema update, component, and registry entry. Solved with validate:content script in CI that fails on mismatch. Lesson: treat content schema as API contract.

questions.question: Round 5 — App freezes on load. Walk me through your debugging process.
questions.sampleAnswer: Reproduce consistently. Open React DevTools Profiler — see render storm. Check Components tree for which component re-renders. Look for useEffect without deps setting state, or setState in render body. Fix deps or move to handler. Verify with Profiler — render count drops.

questions.question: Round 5 — TypeScript error: Property 'id' does not exist on type 'undefined' from useParams. Fix and explain.
questions.whatInterviewerTests: Route param typing from Week 2 — common production bug.
questions.sampleAnswer: useParams returns string | undefined — params may be missing. const { id } = useParams<{ id: string }>(). Guard: if (!id) return <NotFound />. Now TypeScript narrows id to string below the guard.

questions.question: Final — Rate your interview readiness 1–5 and name the one skill you will drill first after graduation.
questions.whatInterviewerTests: Self-awareness and growth mindset — how you improve post-track.
questions.sampleAnswer: I rate myself 3.5 overall — solid on React and TypeScript, 2.5 on machine coding speed. First drill: 60-minute timed builds three times this week. I am starting applications now — real interviews will close the gap faster than more tutorials.
redFlags: Plans to wait months before applying
Cannot name a specific improvement area
